name: Battle of Marignan
description: The Swiss are crushed by France's new king, Francis I (original name). Since then, they stay in their mountains. With their gold. Good for them.

name: Battle of Pavia
description: Francis I is captured by the Spaniards.

name: Temudjin is proclaimed Genghis Khan
description: Means Universal Ruler. A little bit megalomaniac, especially for the leader of such a small trib… oh wait.

name: Noche Triste :'(
description: Hernan Cortes, criminal, flees Tenochtitlan.

name: Battle of Bouvines
description: Philip August defeats both German and English forces.

name: Death of Alexander the Great
description: Weird that such an hated guy dies at such a convenient time… But we swear, it's probably the flu (or COVID-329…)

name: Mythical foundation of Rome
description: Rome wasn't made in a day, and probably not that one either.

name: At the Milvian Bridge, Constantin I becomes Christian
description: After supposed revelation and real battle, Constantin converts to brand-new religion.

name: Lindisfarne ravaged by hairy guys
description: The Vikings enter history as "Northmen". They pillage everything. How rude.

name: Charlemagne proclaimed emperor, but there's already one
description: In a political calculation, the Pope crowns the Frankish king as Emperor, even though there's already an Emperor in Constantinople. Well, it's a woman, so that's probably why.

name: Hugh Capet elected King of the Franks
description: Carolingians out. New dynasty, the Capet, acquires the Throne.

name: Eleanor of Aquitaine, the King of France's ex, marries the King of England
description: 8 weeks after breaking up with the French King, Eleanor wooes the new big guy in the French kingdom: the King of England.

name: Tupac Amaru II revolts against Spaniards
description: Two centuries after the fall of the Incan Empire, a guy says "Hey wait, that's not cool". The revolt will be crushed by the Spaniards and he will be burnt.

name: The Epic of Gilgamesh is written
description: The standard version of this Epic is supposedly written at that time. In short, some king tries to discover a vaccine against death. Spoiler alert: he will not find it, but in the end, the real treasure was the friends he made along the way.

name: Code of Hammurabi issued
description: "Eye for an eye, tooth for a tooth". First evidence of a judiciary text.

name: Foundation of Ur
description: At that time appears, and grows, a city which will shine on all of Sumer: Ur.

name: Marco Polo arrives at the Khan's court
description: The young Venitian merchant is blown away by the mongol ruler, who, thanks to him, will became mythical in Europe and serve as a guide for the Great Discoveries.

name: Jules Caesar meets 23 knives
description: Despite a number of prophecies, the Roman dictator is confident nothing will happen. He is betrayed by everyone and murdered by fearful senators.

name: Nika riots in Constantinople
description: Emperor Justinian almost loses his throne because of some riot incited by chariot racing hooligans, but, thanks to his wife, he clutches on it and maintains his grip.

name: American guys walk on Moon
description: Mission Appollo XIII leaves Earth for the Moon. Or shoots a movie in Hollywood. We don't know. Actually, we do.

name: Sadi Carnot murdered
description: French President Sadi Carnot is murdered by an anarchist guy, because that's the new trend at that time.

name: Felix Faure blown away by best blowjob ever
description: Felix Faure is the first French president to die in the Elysee Palace - after a blowjob too good to be true. Epic.

name: Mercator releases his map, featuring geographical inaccuracies
description: Gerardus Mercator draws his famous map which is still in use today.

name: Foundation of Tenochtitlan
description: An eagle eats a snake, and, seeing that, Aztecs supposedly say "Hey, that's a good place to picnic". It will become their capital.

name: Charles IV dies
description: The last Capet king dies, purpotedly because the malediction of the last Grand Master of the Knights Templars.

name: Battle of Lepanto
description: Against all odds, a bunch of European states (namely Spain, Venice, the Papal States, Genoa, Savoy, and others) band together to defeat the Ottoman Empire.

name: Counter-Amarda fails miserably
description: After "winning" against the Spaniards, the English send a fleet to counter-attach. Because of bad weather, the counter-attack also fails. Morality: don't do Armadas.

name: Potosi mine founded
description: The Spaniards create the mine of Potosi, full of silver.

name: Leif Erikson "discovers" America
description: 500 years before his bro Christopher Colombus, a Viking discovers America - well, obviously he didn't discover it, there were already people there - he called him the ugly ones (Skraelings), because he's a bitch.

name: Mansa Musa destroys Cairo's economy
description: The King of Mali gives so much money while pilgriming that the value of gold falls.

name: Zulu victory in Isandhlwana
description: The Zulu defeat the British. Humiliate them, even.

name: A wooden doll becomes king
description: According to legend, this is more or less the time a wooden doll ruled over the Tui Tonga Empire.

name: Abubakri II fails at crossing the Atlantic
description: A mansa of Mali supposedly went on an expedition to find what lies on the other size of the Ocean. Spoiler alert: it's Brazil. That's probably why he never returned, right?

name: Battle of Red Cliffs
description: An epic battle opposes the three big warlords of that time. Cao Cao is victorious.

name: Ranavalona I becomes queen of Magadascar
description: At 40, the Malagasy queen takes over Madagascar, and will do her best to maintain its independance against France and Britain.

name: Constantin makes Christianism legal
description: After a few centuries of prohibition, consumption of God is legalized in the entire empire.

name: Another fall of Rome
description: Rome fishes for attention by being sacked again. But it's been so many times that no one cares, except future historians.

name: Jesus is born
description: Jesus borns before his birth. Seems that being God's son gives some advantages.

name: Gunpowder mistaken for Elixir of life
description: While trying to discover the cure for death, some Chinese folks actually create the best way to bring death. They will use it for fireworks. Cute.

name: Marco Polo tells all in Devisement du Monde
description: In his cell, Marco Polo bothers his co-prisoner Rutitschello of Pisa with his weird tales of China and everything. The guy is probably a masochist, because he makes a book out of it. It will be a bestseller.

name: Gutenberg mass produces books
description: Young people nowadays, they're always on their printed books, reading. In my time, we had to copy the thing, it took us years. Now it's all easy for them.

name: Affair of the Placards
description: Protestants stick notices everywhere, even on the Royal chamber's door, which shooks him. End of the Protestant hype in France.

name: Day of the Dupes
description: Nothing happens. Still, Richelieu emerges as the new big guy in France, duping the Queen mother.

name: French Red Wedding
description: On Saint-Bartholomew's Day, a few days after the wedding of Protestant King of Navarre and Catholic French princess, a big slaughter happens.

name: Defenestration of Prague II
description: Prague nobles throw Imperial emissaries out of the window. They survive - by landing on a pile of shit - but it will escalate in one of the biggest wars in European history.

name: Roald Amundsen conquers South Pole
description: Facing Scott, Amundsen and his Norwegian team will be the first to arrive at the South pole. Lot of death will happen.

name: Roald Amundsen also conquers North Pole
description: After his adventure in the South, Amundsen becomes the first guy to go to both poles. What a chad.

name: Napoleon III fails miserably in Sedan
description: The French emperor is defeated by a Coalition of German nations. GG Bismarck.

name: Sepoys Not Happy
description: Beginning of the first Sepoy Uprising, the first of many Indian riots against British rule.

name: Rosetta Stone emerges
description: While sieged in Aboukir, French soldiers find a stone full of weird characters and some Greek too.

name: White House becomes Burnt House
description: During the 1812 War, the British army burns down Washington.

name: Discovery of a plot involving three guys named Antonio
description: In Chile, three guys are arrested before they can conspire in order to create an Enlightened republic.

name: Haiti uprises
description: During a voodoo celebration, Haitian slaves launch a revolution which will create the first independant republic in the Caraibes.

name: Harun al Rashid becomes Caliph
description: Harun al Rashid becomes Caliph. He will become legendary thanks to an elephant sent to Charlemagne and the 1001 Nights.

name: Constantinople siege broken
description: Thanks to bad-ass Greek Fire, the Romans in the East crush the Arabs after a two-years siege.

name: Republic of Corsica proclaimed
description: Corsicans riot against their Italian overlords. They become the first Enlightened republic, with a constitution and everything.

name: Vasco da Gama crosses Cape of Good Hope
description: A Portuguese sailor crosses the tip of Africa, and waves hello.
